title: Script R5: Virome Alpha Diversity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4cucq","text":"This protocol outlines our alpha diversity analyses of the virome (from PHACCS) and whole metagenome (from MetaPhlan OTU table). We start by comparing the virome and whole metagenome alpha diversity values, and then look at the differences in virome and whole metagenome diversity between skin sites. Based on the methods from the following publication:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f1dlf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f8966","text":"Hannigan, Geoffrey D., et al. \"The Human Skin Double-Stranded DNA Virome: Topographical and Temporal Diversity, Genetic Enrichment, and Dynamic Associations with the Host Microbiome.\" mBio 6.5 (2015): e01578-15.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":184,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"384di","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

locale: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8h54","text":"## [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ar29l","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1ksuc","text":"## attached base packages: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"32vbr","text":"## [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cii5l","text":"## ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2rdu7","text":"## loaded via a namespace (and not attached): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a1q41","text":"## [1] magrittr_1.5    formatR_1.2    tools_3.2.0    htmltools_0.2.6 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aedgm","text":"## [5] yaml_2.1.13    stringi_0.4-1    rmarkdown_0.7    knitr_1.10.5 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"867eh","text":"## [9] stringr_1.0.0    digest_0.6.8    evaluate_0.7","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Load the required R packages. library(vegan)packageVersion( library(vegan) packageVersion("vegan") library(ggplot2) packageVersion("ggplot2") library(pgirmess) packageVersion("pgirmess") library(plyr) packageVersion("plyr") library(Hmisc) packageVersion("Hmisc") ## [1] '2.3.0' ## [1] '1.0.1' ## [1] '1.6.0' ## [1] '1.8.2' ## Loading required package: grid ## Loading required package: survival ## Loading required package: Formula ## ## Attaching package: 'Hmisc' ## ## The following objects are masked from 'package:plyr': ## ## is.discrete, summarize ## ## The following objects are masked from 'package:base': ## ## format.pval, round.POSIXt, trunc.POSIXt, units ## [1] '3.16.0'
Import the whole microbiome OTU table. INPUT_WM

Check out a summary of the file to see what it looks like. head(INPUT_WM)[,c(1:6)] %s %s ## OTU_ID MG100128 MG100129 MG100130 MG100131 MG100132 ## 1 1 0 0 0 0 0 ## 2 2 0 0 0 0 0 ## 3 3 0 0 0 0 0 ## 4 4 0 0 0 0 10535.1 ## 5 5 0 0 0 0 0 ## 6 6 0 0 0 0 0

Import the virome OTU table. INPUT_PHACCS INPUT_PHACCS <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Alpha_diversity/PHACCS_results_all.txt", header=TRUE, sep="\t") head(INPUT_PHACCS) %s %s ## SampleID SW_Index ## 1 MG100098 11.48738 ## 2 MG100100 11.86418 ## 3 MG100101 11.29502 ## 4 MG100102 12.33014 ## 5 MG100104 12.23747 ## 6 MG100107 12.16315

Import mapping file for whole metagenome and virome. MAP MAP <- read.delim("../../IntermediateOutput/Mapping_files/SkinMet_and_Virome_001_metadata.tsv", sep="\t", header=TRUE) head(MAP)[,c(1:6)] %s %s ## NexteraXT_SampleID NexteraXT_RunName NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID ## 1 MG100151 NexteraXT_007 MG100102 ## 2 MG100150 NexteraXT_007 MG100101 ## 3 MG100149 NexteraXT_007 <NA> ## 4 MG100146 NexteraXT_007 MG100098 ## 5 MG100157 NexteraXT_007 MG100107 ## 6 MG100153 NexteraXT_007 MG100104 ## NexteraXT_Virome_RunName SubjectID TimePoint ## 1 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 2 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 3 <NA> 1 1 ## 4 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 5 NexteraXT_005 1 1 ## 6 NexteraXT_005 1

While the virome PHACCS diversity is included in the output, MetaPhlan only provides OTU relative abundance information, which means the Shannon diversity must be calculated here with Vegan. We also calculate the median diversity and other information required for graphing. This will all be used for the data visualization.

Here we also need to reformat the mapping files. This means only looking at the two time points for which we have a complete data set, as well as excluding the sites and subjects for which we only have partial sampling.
Transpose the whole microbiome matrix. INPUT_WM_NO_FIRST_COL
Calculate alpha diversity for the whole metagenome samples. SHANNON_WM
Merge the mapping file info with the alpha diversity information. MERGE_WM

Calculate median diversity for each individual anatomical location. For error bar calculation, see the boxplot notching formula implemented in ggplot2: http://www.inside-r.org/packages/cran/ggplot2/docs/geom_boxplot SUMRY_WM_MEDIAN %s %s ## Site_Symbol_WM mean_WM IQR_WM N_WM se_WM mean_plus_WM ## 1 Ac 0.7163576 0.8142767 13 0.3568268 1.0731844 ## 2 Ax 0.7493853 0.6449232 13 0.2826138 1.0319992 ## 3 Fh 0.2219632 0.4138806 13 0.1813679 0.4033311 ## 4 Oc 0.3387557 0.4252576 13 0.1863535 0.5251092 ## 5 Pa 1.0617160 1.1723905 13 0.5137569 1.5754730 ## 6 Ra 0.3428010 0.3872246 13 0.1696869 0.5124879

Plot the diversity values as a scatter plot with notch deviation bars. ggplot(SUMRY_MERGE_MEDIAN, aes(x=mean_WM, y=mean_VIR, group=Site_Symbol_WM, colour=Site_Symbol_WM, ymax=mean_plus_VIR, ymin=mean_minus_VIR, xmax=mean_plus_WM, xmin=mean_minus_WM)) + theme_bw() + geom_point(size=3) + geom_errorbar(width=0.025) + geom_errorbarh(height=0.05) + scale_colour_manual(values=c(

We can calculate which samples are significantly different from each other here. This way we can see the statistically significant differences between samples based on bacterial and viral alpha diversity. CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR %s %s ## SampleID SW_Index NexteraXT_SampleID NexteraXT_RunName ## 1 MG100195 9.134844 MG100171 NexteraXT_008 ## 2 MG100199 8.822409 MG100175 NexteraXT_008 ## 3 MG100200 9.908941 MG100176 NexteraXT_008 ## 4 MG100202 10.327899 MG100178 NexteraXT_008 ## 5 MG100204 10.790570 MG100180 NexteraXT_008 ## 6 MG100206 12.246742 MG100182 NexteraXT_008 ## NexteraXT_Virome_RunName SubjectID ## 1 NexteraXT_009 1 ## 2 NexteraXT_009 5 ## 3 NexteraXT_009 6 ## 4 NexteraXT_009 8 ## 5 NexteraXT_009 10 ## 6 NexteraXT_009 12

Run Kruskal-Wallis on virome dataset. kruskalmc(CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$SW_Index, CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Site_Symbol) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s obs.dif critical.dif difference ## Ac-AX 7.210165 52.18821 FALSE ## Ac-Fh 46.603022 52.18821 FALSE ## Ac-Oc 49.900641 54.24178 FALSE ## Ac-Pa 56.049451 52.18821 TRUE ## Ac-Ra 72.081197 52.65148 TRUE ## Ac-Tw 53.299451 52.18821 TRUE ## Ac-Um 2.887960 54.85152 FALSE ## Ax-Fh 39.392857 51.21265 FALSE ## Ax-Oc 42.690476 53.30381 FALSE ## Ax-Pa 48.839286 51.21265 FALSE ## Ax-Ra 64.871032 51.68466 TRUE ## Ax-Tw 46.089286 51.21265 FALSE ## Ax-Um 4.322205 53.92416 FALSE ## Fh-Oc 3.297619 53.30381 FALSE ## Fh-Pa 9.446429 51.21265 FALSE ## Fh-Ra 25.478175 51.68466 FALSE ## Fh-Tw 6.696429 51.21265 FALSE ## Fh-Um 43.715062 53.92416 FALSE ## Oc-Pa 6.148810 53.30381 FALSE ## Oc-Ra 22.180556 53.75747 FALSE ## Oc-Tw 3.398810 53.30381 FALSE ## Oc-Um 47.012681 55.91401 FALSE ## Pa-Ra 16.031746 51.68466 FALSE ## Pa-Tw 2.750000 51.21265 FALSE ## Pa-Um 53.161491 53.92416 FALSE ## Ra-Tw 18.781746 51.68466 FALSE ## Ra-Um 69.193237 54.37264 TRUE ## Tw-Um 50.411491 53.92416 FALSE

Run stats on whole metagenome dataset. CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Site_Symbol %s %s ## SampleID Shannon_div NexteraXT_RunName NexteraXT_Virome_SampleID ## 1 MG100171 0.0000000 NexteraXT_008 MG100195 ## 2 MG100172 0.4522535 NexteraXT_008 MG100196 ## 3 MG100173 0.4903214 NexteraXT_008 MG100197 ## 4 MG100174 0.2945494 NexteraXT_008 MG100198 ## 5 MG100175 0.1339710 NexteraXT_008 MG100199 ## 6 MG100176 1.3291247 NexteraXT_008 MG100200 ## NexteraXT_Virome_RunName SubjectID ## 1 NexteraXT_009 1 ## 2 NexteraXT_009 2 ## 3 NexteraXT_009 3 ## 4 NexteraXT_009 4 ## 5 NexteraXT_009 5 ## 6 NexteraXT_009 6

Plot virome diversity by skin location category. CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR <- MERGE_VIR[-which(MERGE_VIR$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","Neg")), ] CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Type <- "Virome" ggplot(CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR, aes(x=Site_Categories, y=SW_Index)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE)

Run Kruskal-Wallis on dataset by site category. CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Site_Categories CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Site_Categories <- factor(CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Site_Categories) kruskalmc(CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$SW_Index, CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Site_Categories) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s ## obs.dif critical.dif difference ## Intermittently_Moist-Moist 6.775434 25.93003 FALSE ## Intermittently_Moist-Sebaceous 27.249883 25.93003 TRUE ## Moist-Sebaceous 34.025316 23.36625 TRUE
Plot virome diversity by occlusion. ggplot(CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR, aes(x=Occlusion, y=SW_Index)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE)

Run Kruskal-Wallis by occlusion on virome dataset. CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Occlusion CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Occlusion <- factor(CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Occlusion) kruskalmc(CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$SW_Index, CUT_LOC_MERGE_VIR$Occlusion) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s ## obs.dif critical.dif difference ## Exposed-Intermittently_Occluded 50.89856 33.15193 TRUE ## Exposed_Occluded 15.92795 21.74934 FALSE ## Intermittently_Occluded-Occluded 34.97061 32.13919 TRUE

Plot whole metagenome diversity by site category. CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM <- MERGE_WM[-which(MERGE_WM$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","Neg")), ] CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Type <- "Whole_Metagenome" CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$SW_Index <- CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Shannon_div ggplot(CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM, aes(x=Site_Categories, y=Shannon_div)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE)

Run Kruskal-Wallis on whole metagenome dataset by site category. CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Site_Categories CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Site_Categories <- factor(CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Site_Categories) kruskalmc(CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Shannon_div, CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Site_Categories) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s ## obs.dif critical.dif difference ## Intermittently_Moist-Moist 27.57292 29.05167 FALSE ## Intermittently_Moist-Sebaceous 73.75750 28.81832 TRUE ## Moist-Sebaceous 101.33042 25.72345 TRUE

Plot whole metagenome diversity by occlusion. CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM <- MERGE_WM[-which(MERGE_WM$Site_Symbol %in% c("Ba","Ph","Vf","Neg")), ] CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Type <- "Whole_Metagenome" CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$SW_Index <- CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Shannon_div ggplot(CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM, aes(x=Occlusion, y=Shannon_div)) + theme_bw() + geom_boxplot(notch=TRUE)

Run Kruskal-Wallis on whole metagenome datset by occlusion. CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Occlusion CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Occlusion <- factor(CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Occlusion) kruskalmc(CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Occlusion, CUT_LOC_MERGE_WM$Occlusion) ## Multiple comparison test after Kruskal-Wallis ## p.value: 0.05 ## Comparisons %s %s ## obs.dif critical.dif difference ## Exposed-Intermittently_Occluded 64 36.74778 TRUE ## Exposed_Occluded 146 24.14803 TRUE ## Intermittently_Occluded-Occluded 82 35.47290 TRUE
